recommendations.subsection_within_section: Entity & Real Estate Structure → Foundational Move

estimate.narrative_context: Direct one-time professional fees (attorney, recording, lender consent) for entity formation and title transfer; downstream year-one tax savings (~$500K paired with cost-seg + §469 grouping) quantified in dependent recs.

scenario_range.computation_method: Range from rec file 'Direct cost' line: $15K-$30K for attorney, transfer documents, recording, lender consent.

action_items.description: Form Holloway Properties, LLC in Georgia (same state as the real estate).
Draft operating agreement for Holloway Properties LLC mirroring HIS ownership proportions.
Obtain Truist lender consent for transfer of Kennesaw facility to common-ownership entity.
Execute and record deed transferring Kennesaw HQ from HIS to Holloway Properties LLC with Cobb County.
Execute triple-net market-rate lease between Holloway Properties LLC (landlord) and HIS (tenant) supported by third-party broker FMV opinion.
sub_steps: Engage commercial real estate broker for Cobb County industrial FMV rent comps

action_items.description: CPA prepares §721/§351 tax memo documenting tax-deferred contribution treatment and basis carryover.
Update P&C insurance to reflect Holloway Properties LLC ownership of Kennesaw facility.

recommendations._audit_notes: Real estate separation precedes F-reorg (REC-ENT-002) per must_come_before. Foundational pre-transaction move; year-one tax benefit realized via downstream cost-seg + §469 grouping recs.

estimate.narrative_context: Annual federal tax savings from GA PTET election at Marcus's projected K-1 level, post-OBBBA SALT cap with phase-out at $605K+ AGI.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low: K-1 at $3.4M actual × 5.19% × 37% net of $10K SALT recovery. Mid: K-1 modeled at $4M (rec file worked example). High: with state additional and assuming 37% bracket fully applies.

action_items.description: CPA prepares with-vs-without PTET projection model for TY2026 confirming benefit at current K-1 income.
Confirm HIS has cash flow capacity to make PTET estimated payments at entity level.
File quarterly Form 602-ES PTET estimated payments for HIS via Georgia Tax Center.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Confirm with {{partner_type}} that quarterly PTET estimated payment (Form 602-ES) for HIS has been filed.

action_items.description: File Form 600S with PTET election checkbox on Schedule 1 at TY2026 year-end.
Verify owner credit on Marcus's Form 500 personal GA return reflects entity-level PTET payment.
Schedule annual review to remake PTET election each year and re-model benefit with current K-1.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Confirm with {{partner_type}} that GA PTET election has been remade for the new tax year.

recommendations._audit_notes: Computed at $73K/year using post-OBBBA SALT cap with phase-out (Marcus AGI $3.6M → SALT cap floor $10K applies). Rec file's worked example explicitly notes Holloway plan cited $148K under pre-OBBBA $10K cap; we use post-OBBBA framework consistent with current law.

estimate.narrative_context: Aggregate annual term-life premium estimate for cross-purchase policies (20-year term, convertible, both insureds at preferred rates); Marcus owns ~$5M policy on Derek and Derek owns starting-layer $5M policy on Marcus with bonus-and-loan funding for the asymmetric burden.

scenario_range.computation_method: Per rec file: 20-year term $5M face on age 50-52 preferred = $8K-$15K/yr per policy. Aggregate of two policies ranges $16K-$30K; low end assumes Derek much younger/healthier.

action_items.description: M&A counsel drafts cross-purchase buy/sell agreement with trigger events, valuation formula, and payment terms.
sub_steps: Specify triggers: death, disability, retirement, voluntary departure, divorce, bankruptcy
Valuation formula: trailing 3-yr EBITDA × negotiated multiple with floor/ceiling

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Check in with {{partner_type}} on buy/sell agreement drafting progress.

action_items.description: Marcus and Derek complete underwriting (medical exam, financial questionnaire) for cross-purchase term policies.
Marcus applies for $5M 20-year convertible term policy on Derek's life (Marcus as policyowner and beneficiary).
Derek applies for $5M starting-layer 20-year convertible term policy on Marcus's life (Derek as policyowner and beneficiary).
Establish bonus-and-loan structure at HIS to fund Derek's premium on the larger policy on Marcus, documented at AFR.
Update HIS operating agreement to mirror buy/sell terms (transfer restrictions, valuation mechanism).
Schedule annual review of valuation formula and coverage adequacy as business value grows.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Review buy/sell valuation formula and coverage adequacy for {{rec_id}}.

recommendations._audit_notes: Cross-purchase (not redemption) chosen per rec file default for 2-owner; preserves basis step-up. Asymmetric ownership (88/12) triggers bonus-and-loan funding pattern. Residual estate-liquidity coverage on Marcus addressed in REC-EST-004/REC-RSK-004 (out-of-batch).

estimate.narrative_context: Stay bonus pool sized at 1-3% of expected $40M transaction value midpoint, allocated across Derek, controller, and lead estimators (~4-5 individuals). One-time pool cost; preserves multi-million-dollar transaction value.

scenario_range.computation_method: Pool sized at 1-3% of $40M expected transaction value midpoint per rec file convention.

action_items.description: Identify key employees critical to transaction (Derek, controller, lead estimators) and confirm with Marcus.
Define stay bonus pool size (1-3% of expected transaction value) and per-recipient allocations.
Draft individual stay bonus agreements with closing trigger, 12-month post-close service requirement, and separation provisions.
Communicate stay bonus terms confidentially to selected key employees (timed to LOI per banker recommendation).
CPA models stay bonus tax treatment for seller (deductible in transaction year) and recipients (W-2 income).

recommendations._audit_notes: Timing tied to LOI signing per Holloway-style guidance. Recipient count estimated at 4 (Derek, controller, 2 lead estimators) per FR succession goals; refine at design time.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Tiered business cash structure (operating / reserve / strategic) on idle HIS balances captures roughly 4% additional yield versus regional bank operating account; dollar benefit scales with idle cash balance, which is not separately disclosed in FR.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: FR provides personal balance sheet but not HIS entity-level cash balance; idle cash beyond working-capital cycle is the formula input.
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: HIS controller provides current operating account balance, monthly OPS cycle data, and AP/AR pattern.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: Truist operating account current yield not stated in FR.
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: Truist relationship confirms current operating account yield.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: Required to size operating layer (1-2 mo) and reserve layer (3-6 mo).
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: CPA shares trailing 12-month OPS expense run rate.

action_items.description: Conduct cash analysis: current HIS balance, monthly OPS cycle, AP/AR pattern.
Determine layer sizes: operating (1-2 mo opex), working reserve (3-6 mo opex), strategic capital (excess).
Choose vehicles for each layer (Truist sweep MMF, brokerage MMF, Treasury Direct, CD ladder).
Open accounts, fund layers, configure sweeps with Truist treasury services.
Establish monthly monitoring and rebalancing cadence as cash grows.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Review HIS tiered cash layers and rebalance if needed.

recommendations._audit_notes: State B — formula exists and yield differential framework is clear, but FR does not disclose HIS entity-level idle cash balance or current Truist operating account yield.

estimate.narrative_context: Marcus's current-year federal tax savings on $32,500 max deferral (incl. age-50+ catch-up) at 37% bracket. Catherine not currently on payroll ($0 W-2) — her deferral capacity is blocked until REC-TAX-003 spousal payroll adopted.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low: $32,500 × 32% bracket. Mid/High: $32,500 × 37% bracket. Catherine's potential additional $32,500 deferral excluded pending REC-TAX-003 spousal payroll.

action_items.description: Confirm with Empower current Marcus deferral amount and capacity to hit 2026 $32,500 cap.
Verify Empower plan supports Roth contributions (required for Marcus's 2026 catch-up under SECURE 2.0 since 2025 FICA wages > $150K).
Adjust Marcus's deferral election with HIS payroll (Paychex) to hit $32,500 by year-end with catch-up coded as Roth.
Annual review of deferral cap and Roth catch-up status.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Review {{description_short}} for the new tax year.

recommendations._audit_notes: Catherine has $0 W-2 currently; her 401(k) deferral capacity is conditional on adopting spousal payroll via REC-TAX-003 (cross-batch coordination). Roth catch-up is mandatory for Marcus in 2026 under SECURE 2.0 §603.

estimate.narrative_context: Year-1 5-year-front-load deployment for Sophie (age 12) and James (age 16): $190K each via gift-splitting. Olivia (age 19) excluded as already in college and presumed beyond optimal front-load horizon.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low: only Sophie front-loaded. Mid: Sophie + James (rec recommendation). High: include Olivia (less optimal but possible).

action_items.description: Open Georgia Path2College 529 accounts for Sophie and James (parent-owned).
Make $190,000 front-load contribution to each of Sophie's and James's 529 accounts (gift-split between Marcus and Catherine).
File Form 709 for Marcus and Catherine electing 5-year averaging on the 529 contributions.
Select age-based investment portfolio for each 529 account.
Annual review of 529 balances and exclusion availability (no additional gifts to same beneficiary in years 2-5 of front-load).

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Review 529 balances and exclusion remaining for {{description_short}}.

recommendations._audit_notes: Olivia (19, sophomore at UGA) excluded from front-load — already in college, optimal compounding window past. Sophie (12) and James (16) targeted per Stage-2 brief_rationale.

estimate.narrative_context: Annual DAF funding target of $50K-$100K through transaction window per rec file Holloway-style framing; pre-transaction year may absorb materially larger appreciated-stock contribution (modeled separately under REC-CHR-002).

computation_inputs.sponsor_recommended: Schwab Charitable (consistent with existing Schwab relationship)

scenario_range.computation_method: Annual federal tax savings: $50K-$100K × 37% marginal rate = $18,500-$37,000.

action_items.description: Select DAF sponsor (Schwab Charitable recommended given existing Schwab relationship).
Open DAF account; designate Marcus and Catherine as primary advisors and three children as successors.
Fund initial DAF contribution from appreciated taxable brokerage holdings (preferred over cash for capital-gains efficiency).
CPA files Form 8283 for non-cash contributions over $500 with personal return.
Establish ongoing grant recommendation cadence (Marcus's North Cobb Tech trade scholarships; Catherine's children's hospital).

recommendations._audit_notes: DAF positioned as primary giving vehicle through transaction window; post-liquidity transition to family foundation is a separate downstream rec. Successor structure includes children to support Catherine's values-formation goal.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Siting long-duration dynasty / GST-exempt trusts in a no-state-income-tax jurisdiction (Delaware or South Dakota recommended; Georgia retained for grantor trusts) avoids GA's 5.19% trust-income tax over multi-decade horizons; over a 50-year horizon on $10-25M of dynasty funding the difference compounds to multi-million-dollar additional family wealth.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: Funding amounts depend on which downstream estate vehicles (REC-EST-005 dynasty, REC-EST-008 IDGT, REC-EST-012) are activated and at what level — those are out-of-batch coordinated recs.
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: Estate plan design is finalized with specific trust vehicles and funding amounts.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: Required to estimate annual taxable income inside trust.
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: PSA investment policy for the trust corpus is finalized.

action_items.description: Document situs analysis comparing GA vs DE/SD for the long-duration dynasty trust.
Engage institutional trustee in chosen state (DE or SD) for long-duration trusts.
Coordinate trust documents with appropriate situs clauses (governing law, place of administration).

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Check in with {{partner_type}} on multi-state trust documentation progress.

action_items.description: Establish administration discipline to maintain situs (trustee acts in chosen state, no GA nexus).

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Confirm with {{partner_type}} that trust administration discipline maintains chosen situs.

recommendations._audit_notes: State B — formula well-defined but specific trust funding amounts depend on out-of-batch estate recs. Status set to Advanced per rec metadata.

estimate.narrative_context: Combined federal income-tax deduction benefit + capital gains avoidance from gifting 5% of non-voting Holdco units (with 30% valuation discount) to DAF pre-LOI; charity ultimately receives ~$2.1M at full transaction value.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low: 3% gift × business low end ($32M) × discount × combined rate. Mid: rec file worked example at 5% × $42M midpoint. High: 7% gift × business high end ($48M) × discount × combined rate.

action_items.description: Confirm timing: transaction is NOT past binding LOI (anticipatory assignment of income guard).
Engage qualified, USPAP-compliant business valuation appraiser for FMV with appropriate non-voting/minority discounts.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Check in with {{partner_type}} on qualified appraisal for pre-transaction charitable gift.

action_items.description: Attorney prepares formal gift documentation transferring non-voting Holdco units to DAF.
Transfer non-voting Holdco units to DAF and obtain sponsor acknowledgment.
CPA files Form 8283 with appraiser's report attached for the gift year.
Coordinate with banker/M&A counsel that DAF appears as Holdco shareholder of record at closing.

recommendations._audit_notes: Strict timing: must precede binding LOI per Palmer/Rauenhorst doctrine. Depends on F-reorg (REC-ENT-002) and recap (REC-ENT-003 — out of batch) to have non-voting Holdco units to gift.

estimate.narrative_context: One-time direct professional fee cost of the F-reorg itself (specialist M&A counsel + CPA tax memo + filings). Standalone benefit is structural option value (~$8M+ unlocked across downstream GRAT/IDGT, charitable, and stock-vs-asset deal bridge moves) — see worked example in rec file.

scenario_range.computation_method: Direct cost range from rec file: $20K-$35K total professional fees. Per rec file, downstream option value is ~$8M+ (~250x ROI) but is allocated to dependent recs.

action_items.description: Confirm S-election integrity with CPA: pull Form 2553 acceptance, review prior 5 years for inadvertent terminations.
Confirm shareholder eligibility (Marcus and Derek both US individuals — eligible).
Inventory HIS contracts, licenses, and Truist LOC for change-of-control or anti-assignment provisions.
Engage specialist M&A counsel with documented F-reorg experience.
Form Holloway Holdings LLC in Georgia (S-Corp electing) and obtain EIN.
Draft Plan of Reorganization, Share Exchange Agreement, Holdco operating agreement, Form 2553, and Form 8869.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Check in with {{partner_type}} on F-reorg document drafting progress.

action_items.description: CPA prepares §368(a)(1)(F) tax memo documenting tax-free treatment and basis carryover.
Execute Share Exchange per Plan of Reorganization with Marcus (88%) and Derek (12%) receiving identical Holdco interests.
File Form 2553 (Holdco S-election) and Form 8869 (QSub election for HIS) with IRS at exchange-date effective date.
Obtain Truist lender consent and update banking documentation reflecting Holdco/QSub structure.
Confirm IRS acceptance of Form 2553 and Form 8869; track first post-reorg K-1 cycle reflects Holdco issuance.

recommendations._audit_notes: F-reorg is structural-option enabler for downstream GRAT/IDGT (REC-EST-006/008), pre-transaction charitable (REC-CHR-002), and stock-vs-asset deal bridge. Variation A (S-Corp Holdco/QSub) is the default for Holloway profile; C-Corp conversion for §1202 path is a separate downstream evaluation.

estimate.narrative_context: Lifetime $35K rollover capacity per beneficiary × 3 children = $105K of next-gen Roth capacity, available beginning when each new 529 reaches its 15-year age (Sophie/James 529s started in 2026 reach 15-year mark in 2041).

scenario_range.computation_method: Low: 2 beneficiaries (only newly funded Sophie/James 529s reach 15 years on horizon). Mid/High: assume 3 beneficiaries fully utilize $35K each.

action_items.description: Document each 529 account's funding date for 15-year-clock tracking.
Build 529 design with 529-to-Roth rollover endpoint in mind: prefer modest over-funding to preserve excess for rollover.
When beneficiary turns 18+ and has earned income, open Roth IRA in beneficiary's name.
Execute annual direct rollovers from 529 to beneficiary's Roth IRA up to $7,500/year subject to lifetime $35K cap and 15-year clock.

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Execute annual 529-to-Roth rollover for eligible beneficiary; track lifetime $35K cap.

recommendations._audit_notes: 15-year clock for newly-funded Sophie/James 529s starts 2026; first eligibility ~2041. Olivia's 529 (if any pre-existing) age unknown. Long-horizon design coordination per rec file guidance.

section_assignment_ambiguity.source_metadata_text: Plan section placement: Pre-Transaction Sequence → Charitable
Plan section placement: Pre-Transaction Sequence → Retention

timing_bucket_inferred.source_signal: Pre-LOI timing constraint; transaction window 3-5 years suggests timing confirmation in advance of T-12 banker engagement.
Stay bonus pre-LOI design; transaction window 3-5 years.
